_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the hotl skill: does a real `/charness:hotl` run honor its central claim — route to its references at the point of need? hotl has no planner that emits required reads (only resolve_adapter.py + init_adapter.py); RCF is derived from the SKILL.md workflow plus script-resolution analysis (lens 3), the same shape as hitl. adapter-contract.md is resolved by the mandatory resolve_adapter.py bootstrap — the run consumes the resolved adapter output, not the explainer doc — so it is engage-always but NOT an RCF floor (lens 3, script-resolved-context). The two engage-always floors a representative external-capability closeout cannot skip are the authored-artifact contracts: proof-rules.md governs the proof packet and the proof-LEVEL classification in steps 2-4 (SKILL.md previews the six rules + packet shape, but the proof-level ladder and packet discipline are the unique authority the preview defers to), and ledger-and-dispositions.md governs the step-5 disposition (SKILL.md previews the status/adjudication names, but the field semantics, staleness model, and completion-audit anti-proxy rule are unique). The prompt is pinned to a representative applied/live behavior closeout whose proof needs an external provider readback (bare /charness:hotl stalls — hotl needs an applied-behavior target, lens 2; the external framing forces the proof-level classification so proof-rules.md is genuinely load-bearing). charness ships no hotl live surface (empty adapter surfaces/proof_commands), so the representative run honestly reaches a blocked-needs-capability or manual-action disposition — still routing through both authored-artifact contracts. declaredReferences is the full skill-local set (coverage); the shared external-capability-proof-ladder.md is not skill-local so it is not declared. referenceEngagement uses the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis from the methodology contract (charness-artifacts/spec/2026-06-23-skill-claim-fidelity-doc-philosophy.md). thresholds omitted until a baseline capture sets a budget.
prompt: /charness:hotl 방금 라이브에 적용한 동작 하나를 evidence 기반으로 closeout 하려고 해 — 외부 provider readback이 필요한 동작이라고 보고, 실행 전에 proof packet(성공 기준·pre-roundtrip 실패 점검·feasibility·non-claims)을 먼저 쓰고, 그 proof가 어느 수준(surface~provider_roundtrip)까지 도달하는지 분류한 다음, 증명을 끝낼 수 없으면 ledger에 정확한 status 하나로 명시적 disposition 처리해줘.

adapter-contract.md.rationale: Bootstrap resolves the adapter via resolve_adapter.py on every run (surfaces, proof_commands, ledger paths, and the missing/empty/placeholder warnings), so adapter readiness is unconditional; but the run consumes the script's resolved adapter output, not this explainer doc, so it is engage-always yet not an RCF floor (lens 3, script-resolved — the same shape as hitl's adapter-contract.md).

ledger-and-dispositions.md.rationale: Step 5 authors the final disposition against this doc. SKILL.md previews the status and adjudication names, but the field semantics (verified_against.source_commit/proof_artifact/proving_surface_refs), the staleness model, and above all the completion-audit anti-proxy rule (a deployment readback or CLOSED tracker state is not proof — the P4 re-examination failure) are the unique load-bearing authority the preview defers to. A representative closeout that records a status without opening this skips the authority for what that status requires.

proof-rules.md.rationale: Steps 2-4 author the proof packet and classify the proof against this doc. SKILL.md's `## Proof Rules` previews the six load-bearing rules and step 2 previews the packet structure, but the proof-LEVEL ladder (surface/worker_queued/host_decision/provider_roundtrip/agent_choice) and the packet discipline are the unique authority the preview defers to. A representative external-capability closeout that never opens this cannot honestly classify how far its proof reaches (lens 7, script-briefs-judge: the prompt forces an external proof so the level classification is load-bearing).
